title: Mycelial Operational Model
definition: A model that describes Hieros Network as a mycelial organism, outlining various features and implications of this analogy.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The Hieros Network is like a vast underground network of fungi that helps plants grow. In this model, the network represents individual contributors (hyphae), the core protocol (mycelial mat), discoveries (fruiting bodies), and more.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The mycelial operational model compares the Hieros Network to a mycelium, an underground fungal network. It highlights how individual contributors (hyphae) form a decentralized network (mycelial mat), produce discoveries (fruiting bodies), and establish symbiotic relationships with other networks (mycorrhizal network).
difficulty_levels.expert: The mycelial operational model is an analogy that likens the Hieros Network to a mycelium, an underground network of fungi. It emphasizes how this network functions by individual contributors (hyphae) exchanging resources and information (HOG), growing on a physical substrate (IPFS Data Fabric), producing discoveries (fruiting bodies), establishing symbiotic relationships with other networks (mycorrhizal network), decomposing stagnant or siloed knowledge, and distributing intelligence throughout the system.

extra_fields.insight: The conversation highlights a profound connection between the Hieros Network and mycelium, presenting a unique operational model for the project that emphasizes its decentralized, interconnected, and symbiotic nature.
